fr.elements: Main Building; Secondary Building; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness could involve ensuring the single access point is clearly visible and unobstructed, facilitating efficient entry for emergency vehicles and personnel. Consideration for the layout and connectivity between the main and secondary buildings may support smoother navigation across the property. Regular maintenance of the driveway to eliminate any potential hindrances or obstructions could further assist the swift movement of responders. Simplifying movement pathways between the landscaped area and open green spaces may enhance responder mobility, allowing for more effective staging and operational readiness. Identifying distinct zones within the property, with clear demarcations or signage, could improve the swift and efficient navigation necessary during emergency operations, thereby supporting overall response efforts.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Open Terrain; Open Terrain; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Fuel Break; Structure Surrounded by Vegetation; Limited Defensible Space; Single Access Route
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the defensible space by increasing the separation between vegetation and the structure, particularly where vegetation is adjacent, could reduce fuel continuity and improve property preparedness. Managing the tree clusters and scattered trees to preserve open terrain may help to disrupt fire spread pathways. Enhancing the vegetated perimeter to widen existing open fuel breaks can support long-term vegetation management and improve wildfire resilience. Ensuring that the single access route is free from overgrown vegetation and maintains clear space may enhance firefighter access and support suppression activities. Prioritizing the reduction of vegetation density surrounding the structure and maintaining those open spaces could improve defensible space and decrease potential exposure.
